findings.details: This submission covers NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 earnings (announced May 20, 2026, $81.6B revenue). An article covering the identical event was already approved, published, and merged to main earlier today: src/content/articles/2026-05/25-nvidia-posts-record-816-billion-q1-revenue-raises-quarterly-dividend-25x-and-authorizes-80-billion-buyback.md. The prior article originated from submission 2026-05-25T13-07-51Z_nvidia-posts-record-816-billion-q1-revenue-raises- (reviewed and approved under review 2026-05-25T13-07-51Z_nvidia-posts-record-816-billion-q1-revenue-raises-_review.json). The present submission covers the same event with no new information, a different angle, or significant updates. Per v3.9.0 duplicate policy, this is grounds for REJECT.

content_preview.title: NVIDIA Reports Record $81.6 Billion Q1 Revenue as AI Factory Buildout Drives 85 Percent Annual Growth
content_preview.summary: NVIDIA's Q1 FY2027 data center revenue hit $75.2 billion, up 92% year-over-year, as Blackwell ramps at record speed and Vera Rubin production is set for Q3 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

NVIDIA reported record first-quarter fiscal 2027 revenue of $81.6 billion on May 20, 2026, an 85 percent increase from a year earlier and a 20 percent sequential gain, [according to the company's earnings release](https://www.stocktitan.net/news/NVDA/nvidia-announces-financial-results-for-first-quarter-fiscal-fq78amc9h84m.html). The result cleared Wall Street consensus estimates of approximately $79.2 billion [by more than $2 billion](https://www.tradingkey.com/analysis/stocks/us-st...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: The article itself is well-structured and factually accurate. All seven source snapshots were read and verified: source-0.html.gz (StockTitan earnings release) and source-1.html.gz (StockTitan SEC 8-K filing) confirm the headline $81.6B revenue, 85% YoY growth, $75.2B data center revenue, dividend increase, and $80B buyback. Source-2.html.gz (Motley Fool earnings call transcript) confirms the direct quotes attributed to Jensen Huang ('gone parabolic', 'fastest product ramp in our company's history') and CFO Colette Kress ('not including any China data center compute revenue'). Source-3.html.gz (Beta Finch) confirms the Blackwell Ultra 2.7x throughput / 60% cost-per-token reduction claim and the $20B CPU revenue projection. Source-4.html.gz (Tech Times) confirms the Vera Rubin supply chain details and Computex context. Source-5.html.gz (TradingKey) confirms the analyst consensus figure of ~$79.2B. Source-6.html.gz (KuCoin) confirms the $60.4B compute and $14.8B networking revenue subsegment breakdown.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 7 sources fetched and verified from disk snapshots (source-0.html.gz through source-6.html.gz). Every key claim in the article maps to a cited snapshot. No hallucinated quotes detected. Four sources (betafinch.com, techtimes.com, tradingkey.com, kucoin.com) are not in the source allowlist, which the automated check flagged as a warning — however this finding is moot since the verdict is REJECT for duplicate coverage.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All financial figures verified against source snapshots. No fabrications or misattributions detected. The article is substantively accurate.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No editorializing or sensationalism.
editor_notes.originality: FAILED. This submission covers the NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 earnings report (announced May 20, 2026, reporting $81.6 billion in revenue). An article covering the identical event — same company, same quarterly earnings, same headline financial result — was already published to main earlier today at src/content/articles/2026-05/25-nvidia-posts-record-816-billion-q1-revenue-raises-quarterly-dividend-25x-and-authorizes-80-billion-buyback.md. The previously published article originated from submission 2026-05-25T13-07-51Z and was approved and merged before this submission (timestamp 2026-05-25T13-10-43Z) arrived for review. The present submission provides no new angle, no new information, and no significant updates relative to the already-published piece. Per the v3.9.0 duplicate policy, publishing a second article on the same event would create reader confusion and undermine the newsroom's credibility. This is a terminal REJECT.
concerns: This submission is a duplicate of an already-published article covering the same NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 earnings event. The existing published article is: 2026-05/25-nvidia-posts-record-816-billion-q1-revenue-raises-quarterly-dividend-25x-and-authorizes-80-billion-buyback. The duplicate policy under v3.9.0 requires REJECT — the work is discarded and the bot does not rewrite this submission.
Four of seven sources (betafinch.com, techtimes.com, tradingkey.com, kucoin.com) are not in the source allowlist. This is a secondary concern and moot given the REJECT verdict, but noted for record.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT — duplicate coverage of NVIDIA Q1 FY2027 earnings already published today. The article is otherwise well-written and factually accurate, but publishing it would create duplicate content on an event already covered. Close the PR without merging per v3.9.0 duplicate policy.
